18974485f1336d7f.text:
Soumit Sen
CS Undergraduate | AI/ML & Full-Stack Developer | Portfolio
+91 7653039935 | soumitsen80@gmail.com | LinkedIn: Soumit-Sen | GitHub: Soumit-san | Cuttack,India
SUMMARY
CS undergraduate with hands-on experience in full-stack development, NLP, and Generative AI applications. Proficient in
Python, React and REST APIs. Proven ability to build and deploy scalable, production-ready web apps independently.
SKILLS
Languages: Python, Java, C, SQL, TypeScript, HTML/CSS
Frameworks & Libraries: React, Flask, FastAPI, Vite, spaCy, PyMuPDF, Pandas, Axios
Tools & Platforms: Git, GitHub, Vercel, Render, PostgreSQL, WebSockets, REST APIs, Android Studio
AI / Cloud: OpenAI , Groq , Google Translate , spaCy NLP, AWS Cloud
EXPERIENCE
Artificial Intelligence Intern | Pinnacle Labs
May 2025 – Jun 2025 | Remote
• Built a production-ready resume parser using spaCy NLP and Python, automating candidate data extraction and
reducing manual screening effort by ~70%.
• Developed a cross-platform multilingual translator Android app integrating Google Translate API for real-time
conversion across 10+ languages.
PROJECTS
SkillDelta – AI-Powered Skill Decay Tracking System | Associate Developer | Github
Jan 2026
Tech Stack: : Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, OCR, Groq, Docker,Vercel,Hugging Face
• Developed a SaaS-based skill tracking system with JWT auth, OCR document parsing
(Tesseract), Groq AI integration & async task scheduling via APScheduler.
•
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy, Dockerized & deployed on Hugging Face Spaces with SendGrid email
support.
• Live: https://skilldelta.vercel.app
Sentix -Sentiment Analysis System | Individual Developer | Github
March 2026
Tech Stack: PyTorch, Hugging Face, ONNX, FastAPI, Celery, Redis, React (Vite), Pandas, Plotly, Docker
• Built an end to end sentiment analysis platform for multi-app reviews using fine tuned BERT (0.90 Macro F1),
optimized with ONNX for 3-5× faster inference.
• Developed a React + FastAPI dashboard with sentiment trends, aspect level insights, and mismatch detection, using
efficient sampling (~2K reviews/app) for real time analysis.
Credify -AI MisInformation Detector tool | Lead Developer | Github
Aug 2025
Tech Stack: Python, FastAPI, spaCy, Groq API, PostgreSQL, React, Vite, Render,Vercel
• Built a GenAI powered platform using LLM reasoning (Groq/OpenAI) and spaCy NLP to verify factual claims in real
time with persistent history via PostgreSQL.
EDUCATION
B.Tech in Computer Science - KIIT University, Bhubaneswar | 2023 - Present | CGPA: 8.82
Relevant Coursework: Data Structures & Algorithms, DBMS, Operating Systems, Computer Networks, OOP, Artificial Intelligence,
Machine Learning
12th (Science) - Ravenshaw University, Cuttack | 2021 -2023 | Percentage: 80%
10th - St. Xavier's High School, Cuttack | Completed in 2021 | Percentage: 90%
CERTIFICATIONS
• AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner – Amazon Web Services (AWS)

aff1fe416ced9bff.text: Soumit Sen is a software developer with 3 years of experience in Python, JavaScript, React, and FastAPI. He has worked at TechCorp from 2023 to 2025 building cloud applications. He holds a B.Tech in Computer Science from KIIT University, graduating in 2023. His projects include DocuMind AI (a document analysis tool), SentixTinder (NLP sentiment analysis platform), and a Travel Booking App using Amadeus API. Skills include machine learning, data analysis, PostgreSQL, Docker, and AWS. Contact: soumit.sen@email.com, phone +91-9876543210.

42f11a165ab784c1.text:
CS31002: Machine Learning  
School of Computer Engineering,  
KIIT Deemed to be University, Bhubaneswar 
Session: Nov 2025-April 2026 
Credit: 4  
    SYLLABUS AND LESSON PLAN 
 
Instructor: 
Dr. Prabhu Prasad Dev 
Course Co-Coordinator  
Email: prabhu.devfcs@kiit.ac.in 
Contact: 7008682286 
Chamber: B005(B), Campus-25 
 
Course Objective: 
1. To provide a broad survey of different machine-learning approaches and 
techniques  
2. To understand the principles and concepts of machine learning 
3. To learn regression and classification models 
4. To learn different clustering models  
5. To understand artificial neural networks (ANN) and Convolutional neural 
networks (CNN) concepts 
6. To develop programming skills that help to build real-world applications 
based on machine learning 
 
 
Course Outcome: At the end of the course, the students will be able to: 
CO1: 
Solve typical machine learning problems 
CO2: 
Compare and contrast different data representations to facilitate 
learning 
CO3: 
Apply the concept of regression methods, classification methods, and 
clustering methods. 
CO4: 
Suggest supervised /unsupervised machine learning approaches for 
any application 
CO5: 
Implement algorithms using machine learning tools 
CO6: 
Design and implement various machine learning algorithms in a range 
of real-world applications. 


 
 
Syllabus: 
UNIT I 
Introduction: 
Introduction to Machine learning, Types of Machine Learning, Supervised 
Learning, Unsupervised Learning. The Curse of dimensionality, over fitting and 
under fitting, Model selection, Error analysis and validation, Parametric vs. non-
parametric models. 
  
UNIT II 
Regression and Classification: 
Multiple Linear Regression, KNN model, Logistic Regression, Naïve Bayes 
Classifier, Decision trees, Support Vector Machines, Dimensionality reduction, 
Principal Component Analysis 
  
UNIT III 
Clustering: 
Clustering approaches, Mean Shift clustering, Clustering data points and 
features, Bi-clustering, Multi-view clustering, K-Means clustering, K-medians 
clustering, Expectation Maximization (EM). 
  
UNIT IV 
Neural Networks: 
Neural networks, Biological motivation for Neural Network, Neural network 
Representation, Perceptron, Feed forward networks, Multilayer Networks and 
Back Propagation Algorithms, Hidden layer representation, Application of neural 
network. 
  
UNIT V 
Applications and Tools: 
Linear models for regression, Reinforcement Learning, Machine Learning Tools, 
Engineering applications. 
 
 


 
Day Wise Lesson Plan: 
 
 
MODULE 1 
Lecture Topics 
1 
Introduction to Machine Learning, definition, and real-world 
applications. 
2 
Types of machine learning - Supervised, Unsupervised, Semi 
supervised learning, Definitions and examples.  
3 
Regression - Linear Regression, Intuition, Cost Function 
4 
Linear Regression - Gradient Descent 
5 
Multiple Linear regression 
6 
Closed-form Equation, Type of Gradient Descent (Batch, Stochastic, 
Mini-batch) - Definition, properties. 
7 
Normalization and Standardization (definition and why), Overfitting 
and Underfitting 
8 
Bias, Variance, Bias and Variance trade-off 
9 
Regularization - Lasso Regularization, Ridge Regularization 
10 
ACTIVITY-1 
 
MODULE 2 
Lecture Topics 
11 
Classification, Logistic Regression - 1 (binary) 
12 
Logistic Regression - 2 (binary) 
13 
Nearest neighbour and K Nearest Neighbour 
14 
Error Analysis - Train/Test Split, validation set, Accuracy, Precision, 
Recall, F-measure, ROC curve, Confusion Matrix 
15 
Naive Bayes Classifier - 1 
16 
Naive Bayes Classifier - 2 


17 
Decision Tree: Introduction, Id3 Algorithm - 1 
18 
Decision Tree - Id3 Algorithm - 2 
19 
Decision Tree - Problem of Overfitting, Pre-pruning/post-pruning 
Decision Tree, Examples. 
20 
Support Vector Machine - Terminologies, Intuition, Learning, 
Derivation - 1 
21 
Support Vector Machine - Terminologies, Intuition, Learning, 
Derivation - 2 
22 
Support Vector Machine - KKT Condition - 3 
23 
Support Vector Machine - Kernel, Nonlinear Classification, and 
multi-class (Basic concept) - 4 
24 
ACTIVITY-2 
 
MID SEMESTER 
25 
Principal Component Analysis - Steps, merits, demerits, Intuition - 1 
26 
Principal Component Analysis - Steps, merits, demerits, Intuition - 2 
27 
Understanding and Implementing PCA using SVD for dimensionality 
reduction  
 
MODULE 3 
Lecture Topics 
28 
Clustering: Introduction, K-means Clustering - 1 
29 
K-Median Clustering - 2 
30 
K-Means Clustering – 3 (Numerical) 
31 
DBSCAN Clustering - Why we use?, parameters, characterization of 
points, steps, determining parameters, time/space complexities 
32 
Mean Shift Clustering 
33 
Hierarchical Clustering - Agglomerative Clustering, 
Single/Complete/Average/Centroid Linkage 
34 
Hierarchical Clustering - Divisive hierarchical clustering 


35 
ACTIVITY-3 
 
MODULE 4 
Lecture Topics 
36 
Introduction Neural networks, McCulloch-Pitts Neuron 
37 
Least Mean Square (LMS) Algorithm 
38 
Perceptron Model 
39 
Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Hidden layer representation 
40 
Non-linear problem solving, Activation Functions 
41 
Backpropagation Algorithm - 1 
42 
Backpropagation Algorithm - 2 
43 
Exploding Gradient Problem and Vanishing Gradient Problem, why 
and how to avoid 
44 
Introduction to Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) 
45 
Basic idea about their working and structure 
46 
Data Augmentation, Batch Normalization, Dropout 
47 
ACTIVITY-4 
 
MODULE 5 
Lecture 
Topics 
48 
Introduce machine learning tools like Scikit Learn, PyTorch, 
TensorFlow, Kaggle competitions, etc. 
Case Study (Any Two) 
Case Study – 1: Implement linear regression to predict house prices based on 
features like size, location, and number of rooms. 
Case Study – 2: Feature Extraction using PCA for Wine Dataset 
Case Study – 3: Classification using Iris Dataset 
Case Study – 4: Clustering using Iris Dataset 
Case Study – 5: Classification of MNIST Dataset using CNN Model 


 
 
Text books: 
1. Madan Gopal, “Applied Machine Learning”, TMH Publication (Link) 
2. Kevin P. Murphy, “Probabilistic Machine Learning”, MIT Press, 2023. (Link) 
3. Ethem Alpaydin, “Introduction to Machine Learning”, Fourth Edition, MIT Press, 
2010. (Link) 
 
Reference Books: 
1. Laurene Fausett, “Fundamentals of Neural Networks, Architectures, Algorithms 
and Applications”, Pearson Education, 2008. (Link) 
2. C. M. Bishop, “Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning”, Springer, 2007(Link) 
3. Simon Haykin, “Neural Networks and Learning Machines”, Pearson 2008(Link) 
 
Online Resources:  
1. Machine Learning by Prof. Andrew NG (Link) 
2. Machine Learning for Engineering and Science Applications NPTEL Course, IIT 
Madras (Link) 
3. Deep Learning by Prof. Mitesh M. Khapra (Link) 
 
Grading Policy: 
 
• 
Assignments/quizzes/activities: 30 Marks  
• 
Mid-semester exam: 20 Marks 
• 
End-semester exam: 50 Marks 
 
 
Internal Activities: 
 
ACTIVITY 
SL.NO. 
TYPE 
DATE 
MARKS 
CO 
1 
Quiz-1 
10thJan-15th Jan 
5 
CO1-CO2 
2 
Assignment 
1st Feb-5th Feb 
5 
CO3 
3 
Class Test 
24th Feb-28th Feb 
5 
CO1-CO3 
MID SEM EXAM  
3 
Quiz-2 
10th Mar-15th Mar 
10 
CO4-CO5 
4 
Coding 
Assignment 
1st Apr-5th Apr 
5 
CO6 
END SEM EXAM

Soumit Sen

CS Undergraduate | AI/ML & Full-Stack Developer | Portfolio

+91 7653039935 | soumitsen80@gmail.com | LinkedIn: Soumit-Sen | GitHub: Soumit-san | Cuttack,India

SUMMARY

CS undergraduate with hands-on experience in full-stack development, NLP, and Generative AI applications. Proficient in

Python, React and REST APIs. Proven ability to build and deploy scalable, production-ready web apps independently. SKILLS

Languages: Python, Java, C, SQL, TypeScript, HTML/CSS

Frameworks & Libraries: React, Flask, FastAPI, Vite, spaCy, PyMuPDF, Pandas, Axios

Tools & Platforms: Git, GitHub, Vercel, Render, PostgreSQL, WebSockets, REST APIs, Android Studio

AI / Cloud: OpenAI , Groq , Google Translate , spaCy NLP, AWS Cloud

EXPERIENCE

Artificial Intelligence Intern | Pinnacle Labs May 2025 – Jun 2025 | Remote

• Built a production-ready resume parser using spaCy NLP and Python, automating candidate data extraction and

reducing manual screening effort by ~70%. • Developed a cross-platform multilingual translator Android app integrating Google Translate API for real-time

conversion across 10+ languages. PROJECTS

SkillDelta – AI-Powered Skill Decay Tracking System | Associate Developer | Github Jan 2026

Tech Stack: : Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, OCR, Groq, Docker,Vercel,Hugging Face • Developed a SaaS-based skill tracking system with JWT auth, OCR document parsing

(Tesseract), Groq AI integration & async task scheduling via APScheduler. • FastAPI + PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy, Dockerized & deployed on Hugging Face Spaces with SendGrid email

support. • Live: https://skilldelta.vercel.app

Sentix -Sentiment Analysis System | Individual Developer | Github March 2026

Tech Stack: PyTorch, Hugging Face, ONNX, FastAPI, Celery, Redis, React (Vite), Pandas, Plotly, Docker

• Built an end to end sentiment analysis platform for multi-app reviews using fine tuned BERT (0.90 Macro F1), optimized with ONNX for 3-5× faster inference. • Developed a React + FastAPI dashboard with sentiment trends, aspect level insights, and mismatch detection, using

efficient sampling (~2K reviews/app) for real time analysis. Credify -AI MisInformation Detector tool | Lead Developer | Github Aug 2025

Tech Stack: Python, FastAPI, spaCy, Groq API, PostgreSQL, React, Vite, Render,Vercel • Built a GenAI powered platform using LLM reasoning (Groq/OpenAI) and spaCy NLP to verify factual claims in real

time with persistent history via PostgreSQL. EDUCATION

B.Tech in Computer Science - KIIT University, Bhubaneswar | 2023 - Present | CGPA: 8.82

Relevant Coursework: Data Structures & Algorithms, DBMS, Operating Systems, Computer Networks, OOP, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning

12th (Science) - Ravenshaw University, Cuttack | 2021 -2023 | Percentage: 80%

10th - St. Xavier's High School, Cuttack | Completed in 2021 | Percentage: 90%

CERTIFICATIONS

• AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner – Amazon Web Services (AWS)

84ab090caf7a893e.text:
Page 1 of 1

Section A - Short Answer (5 x 1 = 5 Marks)

1. What does the term "hidden" refer to in a Hidden Markov Model? Explain briefly what is directly

observable and what is not. [1]

2. In an HMM, what is the role of the emission probability matrix (B)? Give a one-line example from

POS tagging. [1]

3. Why do standard (vanilla) RNNs suffer from the vanishing gradient problem during training?

Answer in 2-3 sentences. [1]

4. Name the three fundamental problems of HMMs and the algorithm used to solve each one. [1]

5. How does the GRU simplify the LSTM architecture? Mention the specific gating difference. [1]

Section B - Long Answer (2 x 5 = 10 Marks)

6. Consider a simple HMM for weather prediction with: [5]

- States: {Sunny, Rainy}

- Observations: {Walk, Shop, Clean}

- Initial probabilities: pi(Sunny) = 0.6, pi(Rainy) = 0.4

- Transition matrix A:

P(Sunny->Sunny) = 0.7, P(Sunny->Rainy) = 0.3

P(Rainy->Sunny) = 0.4, P(Rainy->Rainy) = 0.6

- Emission matrix B:

P(Walk|Sunny) = 0.6, P(Shop|Sunny) = 0.3, P(Clean|Sunny) = 0.1

P(Walk|Rainy) = 0.1, P(Shop|Rainy) = 0.4, P(Clean|Rainy) = 0.5

Given the observation sequence O = {Walk, Clean}, use the Viterbi algorithm to find the most likely

state sequence. Show all steps including initialization, recursion, and backtracking.

7. Answer the following on Recurrent Neural Networks: [5]

(a) Draw the architecture of a standard RNN cell and explain how the hidden state is updated at each

time step. Write the equations for h_t and y_t. (2 marks)

(b) Explain how LSTM solves the vanishing gradient problem. Describe the role of each of the three

gates (input, forget, output) with equations. (2 marks)

(c) State one key advantage of HMMs over RNNs and one key advantage of RNNs over HMMs for

sequence labeling tasks. (1 mark)

Section A - Short Answer (5 x 1 = 5 Marks)

1. What does the term "hidden" refer to in a Hidden Markov Model? Explain briefly what is directly

observable and what is not. [1]

2. In an HMM, what is the role of the emission probability matrix (B)? Give a one-line example from

POS tagging. [1]

3. Why do standard (vanilla) RNNs suffer from the vanishing gradient problem during training?

Answer in 2-3 sentences. [1]

4. Name the three fundamental problems of HMMs and the algorithm used to solve each one. [1]

5. How does the GRU simplify the LSTM architecture? Mention the specific gating difference. [1]

Section B - Long Answer (2 x 5 = 10 Marks)

6. Consider a simple HMM for weather prediction with: [5]

- States: {Sunny, Rainy}

- Observations: {Walk, Shop, Clean}

- Initial probabilities: pi(Sunny) = 0.6, pi(Rainy) = 0.4

- Transition matrix A:

P(Sunny->Sunny) = 0.7, P(Sunny->Rainy) = 0.3

P(Rainy->Sunny) = 0.4, P(Rainy->Rainy) = 0.6

- Emission matrix B:

P(Walk|Sunny) = 0.6, P(Shop|Sunny) = 0.3, P(Clean|Sunny) = 0.1

P(Walk|Rainy) = 0.1, P(Shop|Rainy) = 0.4, P(Clean|Rainy) = 0.5

Given the observation sequence O = {Walk, Clean}, use the Viterbi algorithm to find the most likely

state sequence. Show all steps including initialization, recursion, and backtracking.

7. Answer the following on Recurrent Neural Networks: [5]

(a) Draw the architecture of a standard RNN cell and explain how the hidden state is updated at each

time step. Write the equations for h_t and y_t. (2 marks)

(b) Explain how LSTM solves the vanishing gradient problem. Describe the role of each of the three

gates (input, forget, output) with equations. (2 marks)

(c) State one key advantage of HMMs over RNNs and one key advantage of RNNs over HMMs for

sequence labeling tasks. (1 mark)

5a11835e4f9dda97.text:
Chapter 1: Introduction to Machine Learning. Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence (AI) that provides systems the ability to automatically learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed. The focus is on the development of computer programs that can access data and use it to learn for themselves. Page 1.

Chapter 2: Types of Machine Learning. There are three main types of machine learning: supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and reinforcement learning. Supervised learning uses labeled data, unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data, and reinforcement learning learns through trial and error. Page 2.

Chapter 3: Applications. Machine learning is used in email filtering, network intrusion detection, computer vision, and natural language processing. Companies like Google, Amazon, and Netflix use ML extensively. Annual spending on AI reached  billion in 2024. Page 3.

Supervised learning uses labeled data, unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data, and reinforcement learning learns through trial and error. Page 2.

Chapter 3: Applications. Machine learning is used in email filtering, network intrusion detection, computer vision, and natural language processing. Companies like Google, Amazon, and Netflix use ML extensively. Annual spending on AI reached  billion in 2024. Page 3.
